formulas.name: The Law Code of Manu
formulas.formula: A set of laws and regulations that governed the society during the Vedic period.
formulas.notes: The Law Code of Manu was an important document that outlined the social hierarchy, laws, and regulations of the Vedic society.

formulas.name: The Concept of Dharma
formulas.formula: Dharma is a set of duties and responsibilities that an individual has towards the society.
formulas.notes: Dharma was an important concept in the Vedic society, and it played a crucial role in shaping the social and cultural norms of the time.

formulas.name: The Varna System
formulas.formula: A system of social classification based on occupation and birth.
formulas.notes: The Varna System was a key feature of the Vedic society, and it divided the society into four main categories: Brahmins, Kshatriyas, Vaishyas, and Shudras.

formulas.name: The Role of the Kings
formulas.formula: The kings were responsible for maintaining law and order, protecting the people, and ensuring the prosperity of the kingdom.
formulas.notes: The kings played a crucial role in the Vedic society, and their actions had a significant impact on the lives of the people.

formulas.name: The Significance of the Gopala Bull Seal
formulas.formula: A symbol of the Harappan civilization, representing the importance of cattle in the economy.
formulas.notes: The Gopala Bull Seal was an important artifact of the Harappan civilization, and it highlighted the significance of cattle in the economy of the time.
